strategy_summary: The binary is vulnerable to a buffer overflow in the 'vulnerable_function' which reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer. We can use this to overwrite the return address and control the execution flow. Since there is no PIE, we can directly use the addresses of the gadgets and functions. We will use the 'pop rdi; ret' gadget to set the argument for 'system' to '/bin/sh'. We need to measure the offset to the return address and find the necessary gadgets.
